name: KYC Drop-off Study
objective: To understand the specific causes (e.g., confusion with document upload, trust issues, technical friction) of user drop-off during the KYC process in your lending app, with the goal of improving KYC completion rates.
audience: Salaried professionals aged 23-35 in Tier 1 and 2 Indian cities, who have started but not completed the KYC process in your app.

questions.intent: To build rapport and understand the user's general tech-savviness and financial habits.
questions.main: To get started, could you tell me a bit about your experience using mobile apps to manage your finances?
questions.probe: What kinds of apps do you use most often?

questions.intent: To get a detailed, step-by-step recall of the user's KYC journey in their own words.
questions.main: Thank you. Now, thinking about our app, could you walk me through your recent experience of trying to complete the identity verification, right from the beginning?
questions.probe: Where were you and what was happening around you when you started the process?
What was the very last thing you did or tried to do before you exited?
questions.intent: To pinpoint the exact moment and cause of user drop-off.
questions.main: At what specific point did you stop the process? Can you describe what was on the screen and what you were thinking at that moment?

questions.probe: What device were you using? Were the documents saved on it already?
questions.intent: To uncover specific friction points related to the document handling and upload steps.
questions.main: Tell me about the process of getting your documents ready and uploading them. What was that like?

questions.probe: How did the actual time it took compare with what you expected?
questions.main: Walk me through your thoughts on the number of steps and the time it took to complete the process.
questions.intent: To gauge the user's perception of the process's length and complexity without leading them.

questions.probe: What, if anything, would have made you feel more comfortable about sharing your data?
questions.intent: To explore trust and data privacy concerns specific to the app experience, without asking a yes/no question.
questions.main: When it came to providing your personal information and documents, what thoughts or feelings came to mind?

questions.probe: Can you recall a specific instruction that was particularly helpful or unhelpful?
questions.main: Thinking about the guidance provided on each screen, how well did it help you understand what you needed to do next?
questions.intent: To assess the effectiveness of the in-app guidance in a more specific and open-ended way.

questions.main: If you had a magic wand, what's the one thing you would change to make this verification process better?
questions.intent: To generate user-centric ideas for improvement by removing constraints.

questions.probe: What were those expectations based on? (e.g., other apps, past experiences)
questions.intent: To understand the user's initial expectations for the KYC process, which helps reveal their mental model.
questions.main: Before you started the verification, what were your expectations for how it would work?
Is there anything else at all that you'd like to share with us that might help us improve the experience?
questions.intent: To provide a final opportunity for the user to share any thoughts or feelings not covered by previous questions.
questions.probe: We really appreciate your honest feedback. Is there any final thought you have for the team?

design_metadata.quality_summary: The final interview guide is robust and aligns with the research objectives. It follows a logical flow, beginning with rapport-building, moving into detailed behavioral recall of the KYC experience, and then exploring emotions, satisfaction, and areas for improvement. All questions have been reviewed and revised to be open-ended, non-leading, and specific, ensuring they are optimized to generate actionable insights from the target audience. The guide is ready for field use.
